content: agent1: implementing 'Add Allow header on 405 responses'. Will touch context.go, mux.go, tree.go, and new file middleware/allowed_methods.go. Will avoid touching other middleware files.

agent1: done with Allow-header feature. My tree.go changes: added var reverseMethodMap map[methodTyp]string and var allMethods []methodTyp (both new top-level vars near methodMap), updated RegisterMethod() to also populate reverseMethodMap/allMethods, added endpoints.allowedMethods() method and methodTypsToStrings() func, and added 2 lines inside findRoute() at the two 'rctx.methodNotAllowed = true' sites (appending to rctx.methodsAllowed). context.go: added Context.methodsAllowed []methodTyp field + reset in Reset(). mux.go: routeHTTP now sets w.Header().Set("Allow", ...) before calling MethodNotAllowedHandler(). If your methodNames/GetMethodName is a separate map keyed differently that's fine - no symbol collision expected, but flag if you were also planning to touch findRoute() or RegisterMethod() since I edited those in-place (not purely additive).
